message:
test(layout): add comprehensive tier system boundary tests

Add unit tests for the Ultra/Mega tier system as specified in ntm-v7sz:

- TestTierForWidthBoundaries: Tests exact boundaries at 239/240 (Ultra)
  and 319/320 (Mega) thresholds
- TestTruncateRunes: 12 test cases for rune-aware truncation including
  emoji, unicode, and edge cases (function was previously untested)
- TestSplitProportionsBoundary: Tests at 119/120 split threshold
- TestUltraProportionsBoundary: Tests at 239/240 ultra threshold
- TestMegaProportionsBoundary: Tests at 319/320 mega threshold
- TestProportionsSmallValues: Edge cases with zero and small inputs
- TestTierConstants: Verifies threshold constants and tier enum values

Closes: ntm-v7sz

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-2e9","title":"Implement stdin and --file support for ntm send","description":"Enable sending prompts from files and stdin pipes.

## What This Task Does

Extend ntm send to accept prompt content from:
1. --file flag: Read prompt from a file
2. stdin: When piped/redirected and no prompt argument given

## Use Cases

From file:
$ ntm send myproject --all --file prompts/review.md

From stdin (pipe):
$ cat error.log | ntm send myproject --cc 'Analyze this error:'
$ git diff | ntm send myproject --all 'Review these changes'

From stdin (redirect):
$ ntm send myproject --all < prompt.txt

Combined with prefix:
$ cat code.py | ntm send myproject --cc --prefix 'Review this code:\n'

## Technical Details

Detection logic:
1. If --file specified, read from file
2. Else if stdin is not a TTY, read from stdin
3. Else use positional prompt argument

Implementation:
func getPromptContent(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) (string, error) {
    if file != "" {
        content, err := os.ReadFile(file)
        return string(content), err
    }
    
    if !isatty.IsTerminal(os.Stdin.Fd()) {
        content, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
        return string(content), err
    }
    
    if len(args) > 1 {
        return args[1], nil
    }
    
    return "", errors.New("no prompt provided")
}

New flags:
--file, -f    Read prompt from file
--prefix      Text to prepend to file/stdin content
--suffix      Text to append to file/stdin content

## Edge Cases

- Empty file: Error with helpful message
- Binary file: Warning, but proceed if forced
- Very large file: Stream rather than load all into memory? (future)
- Encoding: Assume UTF-8

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --file reads prompt from specified path
- [ ] Stdin detected and read when not TTY
- [ ] --prefix and --suffix work with file/stdin
- [ ] Clear error messages for missing file, empty content
- [ ] Existing behavior unchanged when neither used

 {"id":"ntm-2th5","title":"Phase 4: Multi-Provider Support (Codex, Gemini)","description":"# Phase 4: Multi-Provider Support

## What This Is
Extend rotation support from Claude-only to Codex and Gemini.

## Why This is Phase 4 (Not Earlier)
- Claude is the primary use case for the user
- Get Claude working perfectly first
- Codex and Gemini can be added incrementally

## Provider Differences

### Claude Code
- Login: `/login` command
- Exit: `/exit` or Ctrl+C
- Auth patterns: "successfully logged in", "authenticated as"
- Continuation: "continue. Use ultrathink"

### Codex CLI
- Login: `/logout` then restart (no in-session re-auth!)
- Exit: `/exit` or Ctrl+C
- Auth: Happens on startup
- **Key difference**: Codex ALWAYS uses restart strategy

### Gemini CLI
- Login: `/auth` command
- Exit: `/exit` or Ctrl+C
- Auth patterns: TBD (need to research)
- Continuation: TBD

## Implementation

### Provider Interface
```go
type Provider interface {
    Name() string
    LoginCommand() string
    ExitCommand() string
    AuthSuccessPatterns() []string
    ContinuationPrompt() string
    SupportsReauth() bool  // Codex returns false
}

var providers = map[string]Provider{
    "claude": &ClaudeProvider{},
    "codex":  &CodexProvider{},
    "gemini": &GeminiProvider{},
}
```

### Codex Specifics
```go
type CodexProvider struct{}

func (p *CodexProvider) SupportsReauth() bool {
    return false // Codex must always restart
}

func (p *CodexProvider) LoginCommand() string {
    return "/logout" // Then quit and restart
}
```

## Research Needed
- [ ] Gemini CLI auth flow
- [ ] Gemini CLI auth success patterns
- [ ] Gemini continuation prompt (if any)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `ntm rotate --pane=N` works for Codex panes
- [ ] `ntm rotate --pane=N` works for Gemini panes
- [ ] Provider auto-detected from pane title","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:15:59.839364-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T17:11:57.906744-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T17:11:57.906744-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-2th5","depends_on_id":"ntm-qisk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:18:02.100867-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-2th5","depends_on_id":"ntm-t7ol","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:18:02.722254-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-2th5","depends_on_id":"ntm-z3uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:19:26.950863-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-2th5","depends_on_id":"ntm-9f8k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:19:27.36387-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-2th5","depends_on_id":"ntm-cs4z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:35:20.866608-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-egl","title":"Add model alias configuration schema","description":"# Add Model Alias Configuration Schema

## Purpose

Define configuration for model aliases so users can use short names like "opus" instead of full model identifiers.

## Config Schema

```toml
# ~/.config/ntm/config.toml

[models]
# Default models when no specifier given
default_claude = "claude-sonnet-4"
default_codex = "gpt-4"
default_gemini = "gemini-pro"

[models.claude]
opus = "claude-opus-4"
sonnet = "claude-sonnet-4"
haiku = "claude-haiku"
# Custom aliases
architect = "claude-opus-4"
fast = "claude-sonnet-4"

[models.codex]
gpt4 = "gpt-4"
o1 = "o1"
turbo = "gpt-4-turbo"

[models.gemini]
pro = "gemini-pro"
flash = "gemini-flash"
```

## Go Types

```go
type ModelsConfig struct {
    DefaultClaude string                   `toml:"default_claude"`
    DefaultCodex  string                   `toml:"default_codex"`
    DefaultGemini string                   `toml:"default_gemini"`
    Claude        map[string]string        `toml:"claude"`
    Codex         map[string]string        `toml:"codex"`
    Gemini        map[string]string        `toml:"gemini"`
}
```

## Built-in Defaults

If no config exists, use sensible defaults:
```go
var DefaultModels = ModelsConfig{
    DefaultClaude: "claude-sonnet-4",
    DefaultCodex:  "gpt-4",
    DefaultGemini: "gemini-pro",
    Claude: map[string]string{
        "opus":   "claude-opus-4",
        "sonnet": "claude-sonnet-4",
        "haiku":  "claude-haiku",
    },
    // ...
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Config schema defined in internal/config
- [ ] TOML parsing for models section
- [ ] Default values when config missing
- [ ] Lookup function: GetModelName(agentType, alias) string
- [ ] ntm config show displays model aliases
- [ ] Documentation in config init template","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseLake","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:37.096891-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:00:54.464882-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T04:00:54.464882-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-ehcb","title":"Implement account state persistence (JSON file)","description":"# Account State Persistence

Store account runtime state in ~/.config/ntm/account_state.json.

## JSON Schema
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "last_updated": "2025-12-10T15:00:00Z",
  "accounts": {
    "claude": {
      "main@gmail.com": {
        "is_active": true,
        "is_limited": false,
        "limited_at": null,
        "estimated_reset": null,
        "last_used": "2025-12-10T14:30:00Z",
        "quota_percent": 45.2,
        "active_pane_id": "%0"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Implementation
```go
func (r *Registry) Load() error
func (r *Registry) Save() error
func (r *Registry) autoSave()  // Called on state changes
```

## File Locking
Use file locking to prevent concurrent writes from multiple ntm processes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:19:07.344279-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T15:34:31.182429-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T15:34:31.182429-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ehcb","depends_on_id":"ntm-o4nx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:19:26.599052-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
